edges.desc: established and leads the cloister at Dragon's Rest, living there in human guise
serves as leader of the hermits and ascetics at the cloister
has been involved with events on Stormwreck Isle for centuries, including killing a blue dragon there
the adventure begins with Runara's sanctuary being threatened, implying she will interact with the players
The merrow extortionist is described as intimidating and the text instructs the DM to adopt an intimidating voice when speaking as it, suggesting it confronts or threatens the players
is present in the hold demanding tribute
players can use Charisma checks to negotiate a lower tribute amount
players will attack the Merrow Extortionist first, with characters acting in combat order
The players are traveling through the sea on a ship during their adventure
The characters arrive at Dragon's Rest, a haven where world-weary people come to seek peace, reconciliation, and enlightenment
Characters seek out Runara's wisdom and assistance, learning about the dangers facing Stormwreck Isle
The players' ship makes its way toward the calm harbor on the island's north side
The sailors set the players ashore on the rickety dock
The sailors point to the base of the path where the players begin their journey
The players admire the towering statue at the center of the temple from below
shambling zombies are animated corpses of sailors who died at the recent shipwreck site
zombies will move toward and attack the characters if they choose to fight
The players are at the beach, about to leave and start their climb
temple is located in area A5 of Dragon's Rest
The characters escape from zombies and move up the path toward Dragon's Rest
Elder Runara is the leader of Dragon's Rest and welcomes visitors to the cloister
Myla sells equipment from the Basic Rules to the characters during their stay at Dragon's Rest
Runara offers the characters the option to sleep in the monastic cells (area A1) during their stay
The characters eat with the rest of the community in the dining room (area A3)
Myla is one of the residents of Dragon's Rest who interacts with the characters
arrives at the cloister, drawing attention from all residents
She guides the residents of the cloister in their contemplation and study
Runara maintains a secret lair in a cave accessed by an undersea tunnel
The other residents think she lives in the temple at the top of the island
Residents say she's always in the library or checking on other residents
Nine kobolds are utterly devoted to Runara and live at Dragon's Rest, sharing her ideals of justice and compassion
Nine kobolds live at Dragon's Rest, working at night and avoiding sunlight due to sensitivity
Myla was helped by Runara after her wings were injured, and now serves as a tinkerer at Dragon's Rest
Myla is a winged kobold tinkerer who is part of the kobold community at Dragon's Rest
Myla's wings were badly injured in an attack by Stirge, which she describes as 'hungry, icky, blood-sucking, bat-things'
Myla's brothers, Mek and Minn, now follow Sparkrender, the Blue Dragon Wyrmling in Clifftop Observatory
The kobolds of Dragon's Rest are initially friendly toward the adventurers unless otherwise noted
Tarak lives at the cloister where he tends the garden plots and plans to spend the rest of his life atoning for his past
Tarak frequently visits the sea caves to acquire heart cap mushrooms from the myconids that live there
Tarak uses heart cap mushrooms from the sea caves to make Potions of Healing
Tarak is worried about being unable to access the sea caves due to a guardian and may give quests to players (implied by 'Cloister Quests' reference)
spends her twilight years in reflective contemplation at Dragon's Rest
uses mason's tools to maintain the temple
uses mason's tools to maintain other areas of the cloister
can shift her attitude to friendly by engaging in conversation on history, ethics, and impact of individual actions
The path leads up from the shore and widens into the plaza about thirty feet above the bay
shares information about the dragon statue with the players
occupies the second cell in the cloister cells
occupies the third cell in the cloister cells
occupies the fourth cell in the cloister cells, which is cluttered with junk and tools
Characters can operate the winch and lever inside the building
cooks meals in the kitchen/dining area as part of the cloister residents' rotation
the players (as cloister residents) prefer the days when Tarak cooks, though nobody says it out loud
comes to the library to read and discuss various works
comes to the library to read and discuss various works
visits the library and is described as studious among the kobolds
Players enter the library through the oak door and explore the space
helps Runara maintain the temple along with other residents of the cloister
offers prayers and incense to Bahamut at the temple
Adventurers pray to Bahamut at the temple to uphold honor and justice or find courage against threats
zombies have hit Blepp the kobold, leaving him with 2 hit points
zombies overpower the unarmed Tarak if characters don't help
zombies cause trouble at the cloister, attacking Blepp and Tarak
characters can defeat zombies when they first arrive on the island
is eager to reestablish contact with the myconids of the sea caves
given to the players by Tarak
witnessed the most recent ship crash with kobold Rix
suggests the answer to the recent crashes can be found at the older wreck of Compass Rose
several ships have recently crashed on the rocks north of Dragon's Rest
tells the paladin to talk to Tarak about escaping corruption
tells the paladin to talk to Varnoth about escaping violence
Tarak is currently at Dragon's Rest, having escaped there after his lover was killed
promises to direct the wizard to the observatory after the characters help deal with other problems on the island
fears Aidron went to the observatory and suspects evil has arisen there, but dares not go herself
three fume drakes are the guardians of the hot spring pool, initially hidden in the water
the fume drakes guard the hot spring pool and become hostile if anyone attempts to gather mushrooms or enter the water
the fume drakes emerge from the water to attack the players if they attempt to gather mushrooms or enter the hot spring
Characters can bathe in the hot spring waters to regain hit points once per day
hostile toward characters, views them as intruders in its territory
was stranded here after the ship carrying its performing troupe crashed on the northern rocks
considers this area its territory and won't leave it
characters are traveling across the island rather than following the coast
hiding in rocks and light foliage, attempting to ambush travelers
participating in the ambush alongside wingless kobolds
rejects the peaceful teachings of Runara
attempts to ambush the characters while they travel
participates in the ambush against the characters
angry kobolds emerge from hiding places and attack the players
Characters might investigate Sharruth's tomb as part of an adventure
Characters might find hidden vents to access winding tunnels deep into the earth
Characters might access winding tunnels leading deep into the earth
Characters might discover subterranean passages beneath the island
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids who live in the caves
Noxious fumes from Sharruth's tomb are poisoning the myconids and their gardens
Sinensa is the leader of the myconid colony in the caves
Visitors to Seagrow Caves must face the threat of stirges that nest there
The characters are exploring the Seagrow Caves and can take a long rest there, though doing so risks poisoning from toxic fumes
Dragon's Rest has a rowboat the characters can take
This is Tarak's preferred method (of reaching Seagrow Caves)
Myconid adults live in Seagrow Caves and use Rapport Spores to warn visitors to leave
Myconid sprouts live in Seagrow Caves and flee to warn adults of intruders
Sprouts flee toward the nearest adults to warn them of intruders
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids in Seagrow Caves
Sinensa fell ill after going into the crystal cave to investigate the foul odor
Myconids normally avoid the crystal cave because sunlight filters into it, which is unpleasant to them
If the characters attack, the myconids defend themselves
The characters are currently in the myconid caves where they are experiencing the Rapport Spores effect
Players can damage myconids in the caves, triggering their Distress Spores
lurks in the water of the entrance tunnel, regardless of tide state
attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel, including players
created by the myconid leader to keep outsiders away from the tunnel
two Stirge clinging to the tunnel ceiling in the entrance tunnel
if fighting breaks out in this area, the stirges are disturbed and join the battle against the players
Molen and Kraz are spreading fertilizer from area B3 in the fungus farm
Hipsiz and Rugoso tend the mushrooms near the upper pool in the fungus farm
three Violet Fungus grow among the harmless mushrooms in the fungus farm
if characters move into the cave toward the myconids, one violet fungus attacks them with rot-causing tendrils
The players advance into the cave location
characters kill violet fungi with ranged attacks while staying out of their reach due to their slow movement
avoids the characters and the violet fungi
moves quickly to protect the sprouts when aware of danger through combat noise or Distress Spores
agrees to accompany the characters and vouch for them with the rest of the myconid colony after characters defeat violet fungi
A character examines the fungi and notices many mushrooms are sickly, shriveled, and blotched with black patches of decay
Three named sprouts (Bispo, Valup, Popple) work here gathering fertilizer for area B2
Six stirges inhabit a nest in the chamber and will attack if characters move more than 5 feet inside
Sprouts rely on stirge attacks as a distraction to flee toward area B4 when intruders enter
The players are exploring this cave that reeks of rot and has decaying vegetation on the floor
All six myconids are present in the circle chamber, taking turns resting there due to illness and fatigue from the blight
Two myconids (Agric and Omphalo) are tending to the other four (Craterel, Pleuro, Subrufus, and Virosa) who are in a meld trance
The two conscious myconids move quickly to confront any intruders not escorted by other myconids and respond with violence to any threat
Auranta and Enok are tending to the unconscious Sinensa, collecting spores from a glowing red fungus and puffing them around the leader's head to keep them alive
zealously defend their leader and immediately attack intruders who aren't accompanied by other myconids
are present in Sinensa's sanctum tending to their leader
is unconscious and being tended to in their own sanctum
Tarak works in his workshop, which is presumably located at or near the cloister
Tarak creates the elixir of health in his workshop using the ruby morel
Two Fume Drakes lurk amid the sulfurous fumes in the cave and don't leave this cave
The players are currently exploring the crystal cave, observing the environment
released from a stony egg when the orange crystal is broken, now present in the cave
sees the characters only as fuel and attacks them at once upon release
Sinensa is the myconid leader in the Seagrow Caves
Sinensa gives the players the ruby morel and permission to keep treasure after they help the myconids
settled into the wreck of Compass Rose and uses it as its lair
lures ships to crash on the rocky shoal and feasts on the sailors
poses an immediate threat to adventurers exploring the area
shambling zombies from shipwrecks find their way ashore to the rocky coast
menaces the people of Dragon's Rest on the island
some zombies have been trapped in the wreck for years
the ship's hold is part of the wrecked vessel
can be found aboard other recent wrecks as potential dangers
can be found aboard other recent wrecks as potential dangers
holds the secret to freeing Dragon's Rest from the zombie attacks
the characters can take the rowboat to visit the wreck of Compass Rose
the characters arrive at the wreck of Compass Rose
they can tie up the little boat to the derelict's rigging and climb onto the main deck
they're free to explore other possibilities for getting aboard, such as swimming through the hole in the hold
hangs off the starboard side of the main deck of the shipwreck
located at the stern of the shipwreck beneath the water line
The players are exploring the main deck of the wrecked ship
characters climb the 50-foot rope ladder up the swaying mast to reach the crow's nest
at the top of the climb, characters find they are leaning out over the water on the port side of the ship
returns to the shipwreck after looking for other ships to lure onto rocks
two small tiger eye gems found in the crow's nest
one bloodstone gem found in the crow's nest
is located in area C4 on the quarterdeck
characters are present on the quarterdeck interacting with the ship's wheel
two zombies are inside the captain's quarters, either beside the door or shuffling around the cabin
Players have entered the captain's quarters and are exploring the ruined cabin
found in one of the small drawers in the desk
found in one of the small drawers in the desk
found in one of the small drawers in the desk
The players are exploring the galley, observing the scene
worn by the blond-haired man in the portrait found in the crew quarters
contains a hidden dart trap that attacks characters who lift the floorboard
rests in a small compartment beneath the raised floorboard in the crew quarters
is an obvious threat in the lower deck
lurks in the aft part of the hold, waiting to attack
waits to attack characters when they're busy with the zombie, hoping to paralyze and drag one away
is more cunning than zombies and uses them as a distraction for its own attacks
The players have descended to the lower deck and are exploring the area
can be used by players to pry open crates more efficiently
found in a crate containing 20 pounds of whole cloves worth 60 gp
found in a crate, a fine lute with mother-of-pearl inlay worth 50 gp
found in a crate, a spell scroll of command sealed in a leather case
The characters are swimming around in the submerged hold, able to surface for air as needed
The players are exploring the sunken ship hull, having been engulfed by cold water and discovering this undersea world
The pouch contains three turquoise gems worth 10 gp each
The boots of elvenkind are in the chest and can be taken by the players
The pouch containing gold and turquoise is in the chest and can be taken by the players
The players are present at the wreck of the Compass Rose after the shipwreck
The players are at the recent shipwreck site where the Compass Rose was wrecked
Many Undead creatures like Ghoul worship Orcus, the Demon Prince of Undeath
confronts the characters when they show themselves on the main deck
The harpy guards treasure in the crow's nest and will leave if its treasure is returned
Players can attack the harpy, and if reduced to fewer than half its hit points, it might flee
Players can negotiate with the harpy to leave in exchange for returning its treasure from the crow's nest
Runara remembers Brastos was laid to rest in the graveyard atop the cliffs
characters find Aleitha's talisman in the hold of the ship
Players are present on Stormwreck Isle in their dream, observing the island's transformation from darkness to light
If necessary, you can have Runara warn the characters that they're not ready to face this part of the adventure yet
Sparkrender, a blue dragon wyrmling, has made the observatory ruins his home in recent months
Sparkrender and Aidron immediately clashed when they met at the observatory
Sparkrender attempted to turn Aidron against Runara and ultimately overpowered and imprisoned him
Aidron came to the ancient observatory about five days before the characters arrived
Sparkrender imprisoned Aidron in the observatory ruins
Runara battled her blue dragon rival centuries ago, wrecking the observatory
Aidron was under Runara's tutelage at Dragon's Rest before leaving for the observatory
Sparkrender overpowered Aidron in their clash at the observatory
The characters approach the observatory by land and continue toward it
The characters see Sparkrender come to rest on the rocky spire off the coast
strange, twisted protrusions of glassy crystal jutting from the earth on Stormwreck Isle
Players are at the southeastern tip of the island, considering options to approach the ruins
Players are observing the basalt columns with ruins and considering climbing them
Players can attempt to convince hostile kobolds to allow exploration or conversation with a successful Charisma check
are locked in battle with eight stirges at the clifftop observatory
if characters help the kobolds during battle, the kobolds return the favor and become friendly
Mek and Minn are brothers of Myla but left her for dead after a stirge attack that maimed her wings
are present at the clifftop observatory where the battle with stirges occurs
are present at the clifftop observatory where they battle the winged kobolds
if friendly, offer to help characters retrieve the moonstone key from area D1 to activate the bridge to D4
Three kobolds named Ekrash, Erp, and Hev currently inhabit the camp
Two winged kobolds named Nuhro and Snirke currently inhabit the camp
Kobolds are polishing sling bullets, preparing them for use
Winged kobolds are polishing sling bullets, preparing them for use
Players are exploring the isolated study tower where they discover the journal
found in a hidden compartment behind a loose brick in the northwest wall
found in a hidden compartment behind a loose brick in the northwest wall
the potion is contained within the pouch along with 10 gp
Players can choose to fight Sparkrender the blue dragon wyrmling now
Players can free Bronze Dragon Wyrmling (Aidron) from the secret library to gain his help against Sparkrender
If freed, Bronze Dragon Wyrmling (Aidron) will help the players fight Sparkrender
Sparkrender attacks the characters if awakened and not successfully persuaded, or if Aidron or Runara are mentioned
Sparkrender becomes hostile and attacks if characters mention Runara's name
The hidden entrance is located within the clifftop observatory
The secret archive library is accessed by unlocking the hidden entrance through proper statue alignment
The players are exploring the clifftop observatory and interacting with its mechanisms
five pale blue quartz crystals are part of Sparkrender's hoard in the observatory
five blue jasper stones are part of Sparkrender's hoard in the observatory
trapped inside the library by Sparkrender's cave-in, trying to dig his way out
Aidron asks for help defeating Sparkrender and escaping the library
can use the hidden entrance to access the library instead of clearing rubble
The players are exploring the secret archive library, experiencing the stale air and observing the surroundings
found in the library by searching and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check
found in the library by searching and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check
described in appendix A as being in the library
described in appendix A as being in the library
Runara gives each character a potion of healing as a reward
Runara gives each character an exquisite pearl worth 100 gp as a reward
